req.title: Update patient
req.statement: A clinician shall update patient demographics; historical versions must be preserved.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-003 mandates that a clinician can update patient demographics via PUT /Patient/{id}, that each successful update creates a new version, that prior versions remain retrievable, and that a stale versionId in the If-Match header triggers a 409. Four tests are designed: (1) EP/positive — a valid update with correct ETag succeeds, returns 200, increments version, and history endpoint reflects both versions; (2) NEG/negative — submitting a stale versionId yields 409 Conflict; (3) BVA/boundary — updating with a single-character given name (minimal non-empty string) succeeds, probing the lower boundary of the name field; (4) STC — a sequence of two consecutive updates exercises the state machine from v1→v2→v3, verifying all three versions in history.

tests.name: test_update_patient_valid_demographics_succeeds
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_token fixture>
A patient resource exists, created via <create_patient fixture> returning {id, versionId}
actions: Build PUT body with updated family name and current versionId in meta.versionId
expected: Response body meta.versionId is incremented (not equal to original)
GET _history returns Bundle with at least 2 entries
Original version still present in history
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests

def test_update_patient_valid_demographics_succeeds(clinician_token, create_patient, base_url):
    patient_id, v1_id = create_patient
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{v1_id}"',
    }
    body = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": patient_id,
        "meta": {"versionId": v1_id},
        "name": [{"family": "UpdatedFamily", "given": ["Jane"]}],
    }
    put_resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}", json=body, headers=headers
    )
    assert put_resp.status_code == 200, put_resp.text
    new_version_id = put_resp.json()["meta"]["versionId"]
    assert new_version_id != v1_id

    hist_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = hist_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    version_ids = [e["resource"]["meta"]["versionId"] for e in entries]
    assert v1_id in version_ids
    assert new_version_id in version_ids

tests.name: test_update_patient_stale_version_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_token fixture>
A patient resource exists via <create_patient fixture> returning {id, versionId}
A second update has already been applied so the server is now at v2
actions: Perform a first valid update to advance server to v2
Attempt a second PUT using the original v1 versionId in If-Match header
expected: Second PUT response status is 409 Conflict
Response body contains an OperationOutcome indicating version conflict

tests.name: test_update_patient_single_char_given_name_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_token fixture>
A patient resource exists via <create_patient fixture> returning {id, versionId}
Server enforces non-empty given name but has no documented minimum beyond 1 character
actions: Build PUT body setting given name to exactly one character ("A")
Send PUT /Patient/{id} with correct If-Match header
trace: BVA lower boundary for given name string length
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests

def test_update_patient_single_char_given_name_boundary(clinician_token, create_patient, base_url):
    patient_id, v1_id = create_patient
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{v1_id}"',
    }
    body = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": patient_id,
        "meta": {"versionId": v1_id},
        "name": [{"family": "Boundary", "given": ["A"]}],
    }
    put_resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}", json=body, headers=headers
    )
    assert put_resp.status_code == 200, put_resp.text
    new_vid = put_resp.json()["meta"]["versionId"]
    assert new_vid != v1_id

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    given = get_resp.json()["name"][0]["given"][0]
    assert given == "A"

tests.name: test_update_patient_sequential_updates_all_versions_in_history
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_token fixture>
A patient resource exists via <create_patient fixture> returning {id, versionId} — this is v1
Server history endpoint returns all versions
actions: PUT update 1 using v1 versionId → server advances to v2
PUT update 2 using v2 versionId → server advances to v3
GET /Patient/{id}/_history and collect all version IDs
expected: Each successive versionId differs from the previous
History bundle contains exactly 3 entries (v1, v2, v3)
trace: R-HLT-003: new version stored on each update
R-HLT-003: history preserved across multiple transitions
STC: state v1 -> v2 -> v3 fully traversed
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests

def test_update_patient_sequential_updates_all_versions_in_history(
    clinician_token, create_patient, base_url
):
    patient_id, v1_id = create_patient
    auth = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}
    ct = "application/fhir+json"

    def _put(vid, family):
        r = requests.put(
            f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
            json={
                "resourceType": "Patient",
                "id": patient_id,
                "meta": {"versionId": vid},
                "name": [{"family": family}],
            },
            headers={**auth, "Content-Type": ct, "If-Match": f'W/"{vid}"'},
        )
        assert r.status_code == 200, r.text
        return r.json()["meta"]["versionId"]

    v2_id = _put(v1_id, "SecondVersion")
    assert v2_id != v1_id
    v3_id = _put(v2_id, "ThirdVersion")
    assert v3_id not in (v1_id, v2_id)

    hist = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history", headers=auth)
    assert hist.status_code == 200
    entries = hist.json().get("entry", [])
    version_ids = {e["resource"]["meta"]["versionId"] for e in entries}
    assert {v1_id, v2_id, v3_id} == version_ids
